skill.name: vexor
skill.description: Vector-powered CLI for semantic file search with a Claude/Codex skill
skill.summary: Vector-powered CLI tool for semantic file search using natural language queries
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The hardcoded URLs are simply documentation links in the skill description. The flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns refer to the words 'vexor' and 'vector' which are legitimate tool/computer science terms, not cryptographic algorithms. This is a simple documentation-only skill with no executable code or security risks.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged 'vexor' and 'vector' as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are legitimate computer science terms - 'vexor' is the tool name and 'vector' refers to mathematical/data structures, not cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URLs
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md:4 and SKILL.md:22. These are simply documentation links to the source repository in the skill description, not network calls or data exfiltration.

content.user_title: Search Files with Vector-Powered Semantic Search
content.value_statement: Vexor enables natural language file searches using vector embeddings, helping developers locate code and documents through meaning rather than exact keyword matches.
actual_capabilities: Search files using natural language queries instead of exact keywords
Index directories for semantic search capabilities
Retrieve relevant files based on meaning and context
Filter search results by file type and extension
limitations: Does not execute searches directly - requires LLM integration
Cannot access remote repositories without additional setup
Requires initial indexing of files before search can work

use_cases.title: Find code by describing what it does
use_cases.description: Search for code files by describing their functionality rather than function names

use_cases.title: Locate documentation semantically
use_cases.description: Find relevant documentation files by describing the topic you need help with

use_cases.title: Discover related files across projects
use_cases.description: Find files related to a specific feature or concept across multiple directories

prompt_templates.title: Basic Semantic Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use vexor to find files related to [topic or feature description]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to find files but don't know the exact filename

prompt_templates.title: Filter by File Type
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for files about [topic] that are [file type, e.g., Python files]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to narrow results to specific file types

prompt_templates.title: Find Related Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all code files related to [feature] functionality in the project
prompt_templates.scenario: When exploring how a feature is implemented across the codebase

prompt_templates.title: Deep Context Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for files containing information about [concept] and show me how they relate to each other
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to understand the relationships between different parts of your codebase

best_practices: Provide clear, descriptive search queries for better results
Index your codebase before performing semantic searches
Combine semantic search with file type filters for precise results
anti_patterns: Using overly generic search terms that return too many results
Skipping the indexing step which degrades search quality
Relying solely on semantic search without verifying results

faq.question: What is vexor?
faq.answer: Vexor is a vector-powered CLI tool that enables semantic file search using natural language queries instead of exact keyword matching.

faq.question: How does semantic search differ from regular search?
faq.answer: Semantic search finds files based on meaning and context, while regular search requires exact keyword matches.

faq.question: Do I need to set up anything before using vexor?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to index your files first so that vexor can build the vector embeddings for semantic search.

faq.question: Can vexor search remote repositories?
faq.answer: Vexor works with local files. Remote repository access requires additional configuration or cloning the repository locally.

faq.question: What file types does vexor support?
faq.answer: Vexor supports most common file types including code files, text files, markdown, and documentation files.

faq.question: Is vexor safe to use with sensitive files?
faq.answer: Yes, vexor runs locally and does not send your files to any external services unless you explicitly configure it to do so.
